name: Euler Method (explicit first-order ODE solver)
applicable_scenarios: teaching / sanity-check solver for simple initial-value ODEs
python_template:
import numpy as np

def euler_ivp(f, t0, y0, t_end, h):
    ts = [t0]
    ys = [np.asarray(y0, dtype=float)]
    t = t0
    y = ys[0].copy()
    while t < t_end - 1e-12:
        y = y + h * np.asarray(f(t, y), dtype=float)
        t += h
        ts.append(t)
        ys.append(y.copy())
    return np.array(ts), np.array(ys)
typical_cases: pedagogical example before moving to RK4
common_pitfalls: only O(h) accurate — error accumulates fast for long integrations
unstable for stiff systems; use implicit methods (Backward Euler, BDF)
step-size choice is manual; no adaptive control
for real work prefer scipy.integrate.solve_ivp with RK45 / Radau
